name: x402-error-settlement-reused
description: Golden fixture for HTTP 409 X402_SETTLEMENT_REUSED with subcause=nonce_reused (same X-Payment payload submitted across two different request_ids; the second is rejected by the (payer, eip3009_nonce) UNIQUE constraint per ADR-0004).
scenario: attacker (or buggy client) replays a previously-settled X-Payment against a different request_id

error.message: This X-Payment was already settled for request req_x402_replay_first_001 at 2026-05-11T10:00:00Z. Construct a fresh authorization.

next_action.hint: Generate a new EIP-3009 authorization with a fresh nonce, base64-encode the new envelope, and retry.

postconditions: x402_settlements has exactly ONE row across both requests (the second request was rejected at the INSERT ON CONFLICT step BEFORE any facilitator call)
facilitator received exactly one /verify and one /settle (for the first request only)
no double-spend on-chain — the EIP-3009 nonce is also single-use at the asset contract layer; the Hub's UNIQUE constraint is the early defense-in-depth line
